channel.title: AlgoSec — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by AlgoSec (algosec).

item.title: CVE-2025-12381 · 6.1 MEDIUM — Privilege Escalation via Misconfigured Sudoers Entry for Local Users in AlgoSec Firewall Analyzer
item.description: Improper Privilege Management vulnerability in AlgoSec Firewall Analyzer on Linux, 64 bit allows Privilege Escalation, Parameter Injection. A local user with access to the command line may escalate their privileges by abusing the parameters of a comm…

item.title: CVE-2025-12382 · 7.3 HIGH — Path Traversal Allows Remote Code Execution in AlgoSec Firewall Analyzer
item.description: Improper Limitation of a Pathname 'Path Traversal') vulnerability in Algosec Firewall Analyzer on Linux, 64 bit allows an authenticated user to upload files to a restricted directory leading to code injection. This issue affects Algosec Firewall Anal…

item.title: CVE-2023-46596 · 5.1 MEDIUM — Improper input validation in FireFlow’s VisualFlow workflow editor
item.description: Improper input validation in Algosec FireFlow VisualFlow workflow editor via Name, Description and Configuration File field in version A32.20, A32.50, A32.60 permits an attacker to initiate an XSS attack by injecting malicious executable scripts into…

item.title: CVE-2023-46595 · 5.9 MEDIUM — Net-NTLM leak via HTML injection in FireFlow VisualFlow workflow editor
item.description: Net-NTLM leak via HTML injection in FireFlow VisualFlow workflow editor allows an attacker to obtain victim’s domain credentials and Net-NTLM hash which can lead to relay domain attacks. Fixed in A32.20 (b570 or above), A32.50 (b390 or above)
